tEn: WebGPT: Browser-assisted question-answering with human feedback
tZh: WebGPT：基于浏览器辅助的问答系统与人类反馈优化
sEn: WebGPT fine-tunes GPT-3 to answer questions by browsing the web, achieving human-preferred answers 56% of the time over human demonstrators.
sZh: WebGPT通过微调GPT-3使其能在文本浏览器中搜索和导航网页来回答长问题，结合模仿学习和人类反馈优化答案质量，其答案在56%的情况下比人类演示者更受青睐。
absEn: We fine-tune GPT-3 to answer long-form questions using a text-based web-browsing environment, which allows the model to search and navigate the web. By setting up the task so that it can be performed by humans, we are able to train models on the task using imitation learning, and then optimize answer quality with human feedback. To make human evaluation of factual accuracy easier, models must collect references while browsing in support of their answers. We train and evaluate our models on ELI5, a dataset of questions asked by Reddit users. Our best model is obtained by fine-tuning GPT-3 using behavior cloning, and then performing rejection sampling against a reward model trained to predict human preferences. This model's answers are preferred by humans 56% of the time to those of our human demonstrators, and 69% of the time to the highest-voted answer from Reddit.
absZh: 我们通过基于文本的网页浏览环境微调GPT-3，使其能够搜索和浏览网页来回答长问题。通过设置可由人类执行的任务，我们能够使用模仿学习训练模型，然后通过人类反馈优化答案质量。为了使事实准确性的人工评估更容易，模型在浏览时必须收集引用以支持其答案。我们在ELI5（Reddit用户提出的问题数据集）上训练和评估模型。我们最好的模型是通过行为克隆微调GPT-3，然后对预测人类偏好的奖励模型进行拒绝采样得到的。该模型的答案在56%的情况下比人类演示者的答案更受人类青睐，在69%的情况下比Reddit上最高赞的答案更受欢迎。

contrib.en: Fine-tunes GPT-3 to answer long-form questions using a text-based web-browsing environment.
contrib.zh: 微调GPT-3以使用基于文本的网络浏览环境回答长形式问题。

contrib.en: Collects human demonstrations and comparisons to train via imitation learning and human feedback.
contrib.zh: 收集人类示范和比较，通过模仿学习和人类反馈进行训练。

contrib.en: Requires models to collect references during browsing to aid factual accuracy evaluation.
contrib.zh: 要求模型在浏览过程中收集参考文献以帮助评估事实准确性。

contrib.en: Achieves human-level performance on ELI5, with answers preferred 56% over human demonstrators and 69% over Reddit top answers.
contrib.zh: 在ELI5上达到人类水平，答案比人类示范者偏好56%，比Reddit最高票答案偏好69%。

contrib.en: Outperforms GPT-3 on TruthfulQA, achieving 75% truthful and 54% truthful and informative answers.
contrib.zh: 在TruthfulQA上优于GPT-3，达到75%真实和54%真实且信息丰富的答案。

contrib.en: Analyzes scaling trends with dataset size, parameter count, and inference-time compute for rejection sampling.
contrib.zh: 分析了数据集大小、参数数量和推理时算力对拒绝采样的缩放趋势。

limits.en: Struggles with out-of-distribution questions, as shown by lower performance on TruthfulQA compared to humans.
limits.zh: 在分布外问题上表现不佳，如在TruthfulQA上性能低于人类。

limits.en: May cherry-pick references to appear convincing, not reflecting fair evidence assessment.
limits.zh: 可能挑选参考文献以显得有说服力，而非反映公平的证据评估。

limits.en: Perpetuates biases from GPT-3 and existing sources, potentially reinforcing confirmation bias.
limits.zh: 延续GPT-3和现有来源的偏见，可能强化确认偏误。

limits.en: Risk of overreliance due to authoritative appearance with citations, despite occasional falsehoods.
limits.zh: 由于引用显得权威，存在过度依赖风险，尽管偶尔有错误陈述。

limits.en: Requires careful human oversight for safety, especially with more capable models accessing the web.
limits.zh: 需要谨慎的人类监督以确保安全，尤其是更强大的模型访问网络时。

items.en: We fine-tune GPT-3 to answer long-form questions using a text-based web-browsing environment, which allows the model to search and navigate the web. By setting up the task so that it can be performed by humans, we are able to train models on the task using imitation learning, and then optimize answer quality with human feedback. To make human evaluation of factual accuracy easier, models must collect references while browsing in support of their answers. We train and evaluate our models on ELI5, a dataset of questions asked by Reddit users. Our best model is obtained by fine-tuning GPT-3 using behavior cloning, and then performing rejection sampling against a reward model trained to predict human preferences. This model’s answers are preferred by humans 56% of the time to those of our human demonstrators, and 69% of the time to the highest-voted answer from Reddit.
items.zh: 我们微调GPT-3，使其能够通过基于文本的网页浏览环境回答长文问题，该环境允许模型搜索和浏览网页。通过将任务设置为人类可执行的形式，我们能够使用模仿学习在任务上训练模型，然后通过人类反馈优化答案质量。为了使事实准确性的人工评估更容易，模型在浏览时必须收集支持其答案的参考文献。我们在ELI5（一个由Reddit用户提出的问题数据集）上训练和评估我们的模型。我们最好的模型是通过使用行为克隆微调GPT-3，然后针对一个训练用于预测人类偏好的奖励模型进行拒绝采样得到的。该模型的答案在56%的情况下被人类认为优于我们的人类演示者，在69%的情况下优于Reddit上最高赞的答案。

items.en: A rising challenge in NLP is long-form question-answering (LFQA), in which a paragraph-length answer is generated in response to an open-ended question. LFQA systems have the potential to become one of the main ways people learn about the world, but currently lag behind human performance (Krishna et al., 2021). Existing work tends to focus on two core components of the task, information retrieval and synthesis.
items.zh: NLP中一个日益严峻的挑战是长文问答（LFQA），即针对开放式问题生成段落长度的答案。LFQA系统有潜力成为人们了解世界的主要方式之一，但目前仍落后于人类表现（Krishna等人，2021）。现有工作往往侧重于任务的两个核心组成部分：信息检索和综合。

items.en: In this work we leverage existing solutions to these components: we outsource document retrieval to the Microsoft Bing Web Search API,111https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/bing/apis/bing-web-search-api and utilize unsupervised pre-training to achieve high-quality synthesis by fine-tuning GPT-3 (Brown et al., 2020). Instead of trying to improve these ingredients, we focus on combining them using more faithful training objectives. Following Stiennon et al. (2020), we use human feedback to directly optimize answer quality, allowing us to achieve performance competitive with humans.
items.zh: 在这项工作中，我们利用这些组成部分的现有解决方案：我们将文档检索外包给微软必应网页搜索API，并利用无监督预训练通过微调GPT-3实现高质量的综合（Brown等人，2020）。我们不是试图改进这些要素，而是专注于使用更忠实的训练目标将它们结合起来。遵循Stiennon等人（2020）的工作，我们使用人类反馈直接优化答案质量，从而获得与人类竞争的性能。

items.en: We create a text-based web-browsing environment that a fine-tuned language model can interact with. This allows us to improve both retrieval and synthesis in an end-to-end fashion using general methods such as imitation learning and reinforcement learning.
items.zh: 我们创建了一个基于文本的网页浏览环境，微调后的语言模型可以与之交互。这使我们能够使用通用方法（如模仿学习和强化学习）以端到端的方式改进检索和综合。

items.en: We generate answers with references: passages extracted by the model from web pages while browsing. This is crucial for allowing labelers to judge the factual accuracy of answers, without engaging in a difficult and subjective process of independent research.
items.zh: 我们生成带有参考文献的答案：模型在浏览时从网页中提取的段落。这对于让标注者判断答案的事实准确性至关重要，而无需进行困难且主观的独立研究过程。

items.en: Our models are trained primarily to answer questions from ELI5 (Fan et al., 2019), a dataset of questions taken from the “Explain Like I’m Five” subreddit. We collect two additional kinds of data: demonstrations of humans using our web-browsing environment to answer questions, and comparisons between two model-generated answers to the same question (each with their own set of references). Answers are judged for their factual accuracy, coherence, and overall usefulness.
items.zh: 我们的模型主要训练用于回答ELI5（Fan等人，2019）中的问题，该数据集来自“Explain Like I'm Five”子版块的问题。我们收集了另外两种数据：人类使用我们的网页浏览环境回答问题的演示，以及针对同一问题两个模型生成答案之间的比较（每个答案带有各自的参考文献）。答案根据其事实准确性、连贯性和整体有用性进行评判。

items.en: We use this data in four main ways: behavior cloning (i.e., supervised fine-tuning) using the demonstrations, reward modeling using the comparisons, reinforcement learning against the reward model, and rejection sampling against the reward model. Our best model uses a combination of behavior cloning and rejection sampling. We also find reinforcement learning to provide some benefit when inference-time compute is more limited.
items.zh: 我们以四种主要方式使用这些数据：使用演示进行行为克隆（即监督微调），使用比较进行奖励建模，针对奖励模型进行强化学习，以及针对奖励模型进行拒绝采样。我们最好的模型结合了行为克隆和拒绝采样。我们还发现，当推理时算力更有限时，强化学习也能提供一些好处。

items.en: We evaluate our best model in three different ways. First, we compare our model’s answers to answers written by our human demonstrators on a held-out set of questions. Our model’s answers are preferred 56% of the time, demonstrating human-level usage of the text-based browser. Second, we compare our model’s answers (with references stripped, for fairness) to the highest-voted answer provided by the ELI5 dataset. Our model’s answers are preferred 69% of the time. Third, we evaluate our model on TruthfulQA (Lin et al., 2021), an adversarial dataset of short-form questions. Our model’s answers are true 75% of the time, and are both true and informative 54% of the time, outperforming our base model (GPT-3), but falling short of human performance.
items.zh: 我们以三种不同方式评估我们最好的模型。首先，我们将模型的答案与人类演示者在保留问题集上撰写的答案进行比较。我们的模型答案在56%的情况下被优先选择，展示了基于文本浏览器的类人使用水平。其次，我们将模型的答案（为公平起见去掉参考文献）与ELI5数据集中最高赞答案进行比较。我们的模型答案在69%的情况下被优先选择。第三，我们在TruthfulQA（Lin等人，2021）上评估我们的模型，这是一个对抗性的短问题数据集。我们的模型答案在75%的情况下是真实的，在54%的情况下既真实又有信息量，优于我们的基础模型（GPT-3），但未达到人类表现。

items.en: The remainder of the paper is structured as follows:
items.zh: 本文其余部分结构如下：

items.en: In Section 2, we describe our text-based web-browsing environment and how our models interact with it.
items.zh: 在第2节中，我们描述了我们基于文本的网页浏览环境以及我们的模型如何与之交互。

items.en: In Section 3, we explain our data collection and training methods in more detail.
items.zh: 在第3节中，我们更详细地解释我们的数据收集和训练方法。

items.en: In Section 4, we evaluate our best-performing models (for different inference-time compute budgets) on ELI5 and TruthfulQA.
items.zh: 在第4节中，我们评估我们表现最好的模型（针对不同的推理时算力预算）在ELI5和TruthfulQA上的表现。

items.en: In Section 5, we provide experimental results comparing our different methods and how they scale with dataset size, parameter count, and inference-time compute.
items.zh: 在第5节中，我们提供实验结果，比较我们的不同方法以及它们如何随数据集大小、参数数量和推理时算力扩展。

items.en: In Section 6, we discuss the implications of our findings for training models to answer questions truthfully, and broader impacts.
items.zh: 在第6节中，我们讨论我们的发现对训练模型真实回答问题的影响以及更广泛的影响。

items.en: Previous work on question-answering such as REALM (Guu et al., 2020) and RAG (Lewis et al., 2020a) has focused on improving document retrieval for a given query. Instead, we use a familiar existing method for this: a modern search engine (Bing). This has two main advantages. First, modern search engines are already very powerful, and index a large number of up-to-date documents. Second, it allows us to focus on the higher-level task of using a search engine to answer questions, something that humans can do well, and that a language model can mimic.
items.zh: 先前关于问答的工作，如REALM（Guu等人，2020）和RAG（Lewis等人，2020a），侧重于改进给定查询的文档检索。相反，我们使用一种熟悉的现有方法：现代搜索引擎（必应）。这有两个主要优点。首先，现代搜索引擎已经非常强大，索引了大量最新文档。其次，它使我们能够专注于使用搜索引擎回答问题的更高层次任务，这是人类可以很好完成且语言模型可以模仿的。

items.en: For this approach, we designed a text-based web-browsing environment. The language model is prompted with a written summary of the current state of the environment, including the question, the text of the current page at the current cursor location, and some other information (see Figure 1(1(b))). In response to this, the model must issue one of the commands given in Table 1, which performs an action such as running a Bing search, clicking on a link, or scrolling around. This process is then repeated with a fresh context (hence, the only memory of previous steps is what is recorded in the summary).
items.zh: 对于这种方法，我们设计了一个基于文本的网页浏览环境。语言模型被提示当前环境状态的书面摘要，包括问题、当前页面在当前光标位置的文本以及其他一些信息（见图1(1(b))）。作为回应，模型必须发出表1中给出的一个命令，该命令执行诸如运行必应搜索、点击链接或滚动等操作。然后，这个过程用新的上下文重复（因此，先前步骤的唯一记忆是记录在摘要中的内容）。

items.en: While the model is browsing, one of the actions it can take is to quote an extract from the current page. When this is performed, the page title, domain name and extract are recorded to be used later as a reference. Browsing then continues until either the model issues a command to end browsing, the maximum number of actions has been reached, or the maximum total length of references has been reached. At this point, as long as there is at least one reference, the model is prompted with the question and the references, and must compose its final answer.
items.zh: 在模型浏览时，它可以执行的一个操作是从当前页面引用一段摘录。当执行此操作时，页面标题、域名和摘录被记录下来，稍后用作参考文献。然后浏览继续，直到模型发出结束浏览的命令、达到最大操作次数或达到参考文献的最大总长度。此时，只要至少有一个参考文献，模型就会被提示问题和参考文献，并必须撰写最终答案。

items.en: Further technical details about our environment can be found in Appendix A.
items.zh: 关于我们环境的更多技术细节可以在附录A中找到。

items.en: Guidance from humans is central to our approach. A language model pre-trained on natural language would not be able to use our text-based browser, since it does not know the format of valid commands. We therefore collected examples of humans using the browser to answer questions, which we call demonstrations. However, training on demonstrations alone does not directly optimize answer quality, and is unlikely to lead far beyond human performance (Stiennon et al., 2020). We therefore collected pairs of model-generated answers to the same question, and asked humans which one they preferred, which we call comparisons.
items.zh: 人类的指导是我们方法的核心。一个在自然语言上预训练的语言模型无法使用我们的基于文本的浏览器，因为它不知道有效命令的格式。因此，我们收集了人类使用浏览器回答问题的示例，我们称之为演示。然而，仅靠演示训练并不能直接优化答案质量，并且不太可能大幅超越人类表现（Stiennon等人，2020）。因此，我们收集了针对同一问题的模型生成答案对，并询问人类更喜欢哪一个，我们称之为比较。

items.en: For both demonstrations and comparisons, the vast majority of questions were taken from ELI5 (Fan et al., 2019), a dataset of long-form questions. For diversity and experimentation, we also mixed in a small number of questions from other sources, such as TriviaQA (Joshi et al., 2017). In total, we collected around 6,000 demonstrations, 92% of which were for questions from ELI5, and around 21,500 comparisons, 98% of which were for questions from ELI5. A more detailed breakdown of the questions we used along with post-processing details can be found in Appendix B.
items.zh: 在演示和比较中，绝大多数问题取自ELI5（Fan等人，2019），这是一个长篇问题数据集。为了多样性和实验，我们还混合了少量来自其他来源的问题，例如TriviaQA（Joshi等人，2017）。总共收集了约6000个演示，其中92%来自ELI5问题，以及约21500个比较，其中98%来自ELI5问题。我们使用的问题的详细分类以及后处理细节见附录B。

items.en: To make it easier for humans to provide demonstrations, we designed a graphical user interface for the environment (see Figure 1(1(a))). This displays essentially the same information as the text-based interface and allows any valid action to be performed, but is more human-friendly. For comparisons, we designed a similar interface, allowing auxiliary annotations as well as comparison ratings to be provided, although only the final comparison ratings (better, worse or equally good overall) were used in training.
items.zh: 为了方便人类提供演示，我们为环境设计了一个图形用户界面（见图1(1(a))）。该界面显示的信息与基于文本的界面基本相同，并允许执行任何有效操作，但更人性化。对于比较，我们设计了一个类似的界面，允许提供辅助注释以及比较评分，尽管只有最终的比较评分（整体更好、更差或同样好）用于训练。

items.en: For both demonstrations and comparisons, we emphasized that answers should be relevant, coherent, and supported by trustworthy references. Further details about these criteria and other aspects of our data collection pipeline can be found in Appendix C.
items.zh: 在演示和比较中，我们强调答案应相关、连贯，并得到可信参考文献的支持。关于这些标准以及数据收集流程其他方面的更多细节见附录C。

items.en: We are releasing a dataset of comparisons, the details of which can be found in Appendix K.
items.zh: 我们发布了一个比较数据集，其细节见附录K。

items.en: The use of pre-trained models is crucial to our approach. Many of the underlying capabilities required to successfully use our environment to answer questions, such as reading comprehension and answer synthesis, emerge as zero-shot capabilities of language models (Brown et al., 2020). We therefore fine-tuned models from the GPT-3 model family, focusing on the 760M, 13B and 175B model sizes.
items.zh: 使用预训练模型对我们的方法至关重要。成功使用我们的环境回答问题所需的许多底层能力，如阅读理解和答案综合，作为语言模型的零样本能力出现（Brown等人，2020）。因此，我们从GPT-3模型家族微调模型，重点关注760M、13B和175B模型规模。

items.en: Starting from these models, we used four main training methods:
items.zh: 从这些模型开始，我们使用了四种主要训练方法：

items.en: Behavior cloning (BC). We fine-tuned on the demonstrations using supervised learning, with the commands issued by the human demonstrators as labels.
items.zh: 行为克隆（BC）。我们使用监督学习在演示上进行微调，以人类演示者发出的命令作为标签。

items.en: Reward modeling (RM). Starting from the BC model with the final unembedding layer removed, we trained a model to take in a question and an answer with references, and output a scalar reward. Following Stiennon et al. (2020), the reward represents an Elo score, scaled such that the difference between two scores represents the logit of the probability that one will be preferred to the other by the human labelers. The reward model is trained using a cross-entropy loss, with the comparisons as labels. Ties are treated as soft 50% labels.
items.zh: 奖励建模（RM）。从移除最终解嵌入层的BC模型开始，我们训练一个模型，输入问题和带参考文献的答案，输出标量奖励。遵循Stiennon等人（2020），奖励表示Elo分数，经过缩放使得两个分数之差代表人类标注者偏好其中一个的对数几率。奖励模型使用交叉熵损失训练，以比较作为标签。平局被视为50%的软标签。

items.en: Reinforcement learning (RL). Once again following Stiennon et al. (2020), we fine-tuned the BC model on our environment using PPO (Schulman et al., 2017). For the environment reward, we took the reward model score at the end of each episode, and added this to a KL penalty from the BC model at each token to mitigate overoptimization of the reward model.
items.zh: 强化学习（RL）。再次遵循Stiennon等人（2020），我们使用PPO（Schulman等人，2017）在我们的环境上微调BC模型。对于环境奖励，我们在每个回合结束时取奖励模型分数，并将其添加到来自BC模型的每个token的KL惩罚中，以减轻奖励模型的过度优化。

items.en: Rejection sampling (best-of-n). We sampled a fixed number of answers (4, 16 or 64) from either the BC model or the RL model (if left unspecified, we used the BC model), and selected the one that was ranked highest by the reward model. We used this as an alternative method of optimizing against the reward model, which requires no additional training, but instead uses more inference-time compute.
items.zh: 拒绝采样（best-of-n）。我们从BC模型或RL模型（如果未指定，则使用BC模型）中采样固定数量的答案（4、16或64），并选择奖励模型排名最高的答案。我们将其作为针对奖励模型优化的替代方法，无需额外训练，但使用更多推理时算力。

items.en: We used mutually disjoint sets of questions for each of BC, RM and RL.
items.zh: 我们为BC、RM和RL使用了互不相交的问题集。

items.en: For BC, we held out around 4% of the demonstrations to use as a validation set.
items.zh: 对于BC，我们保留了约4%的演示作为验证集。

items.en: For RM, we sampled answers for the comparison datasets in an ad-hoc manner, using models of various sizes (but primarily the 175B model size), trained using various combinations of methods and hyperparameters, and combined them into a single dataset. This was for data efficiency: we collected many comparisons for evaluation purposes, such as for tuning hyperparameters, and did not want to waste this data. Our final reward models were trained on around 16,000 comparisons, the remaining 5,500 being used for evaluation only.
items.zh: 对于RM，我们以临时方式为比较数据集采样答案，使用各种规模的模型（但主要是175B模型规模），使用各种方法和超参数组合进行训练，并将它们合并到单个数据集中。这是为了数据效率：我们收集了许多用于评估目的的比较，例如用于调整超参数，并且不想浪费这些数据。我们的最终奖励模型在约16000个比较上训练，剩余5500个仅用于评估。

items.en: For RL, we trained on a mixture of 90% questions from ELI5 and 10% questions from TriviaQA. To improve sample efficiency, at the end of each episode we inserted 15 additional answering-only episodes using the same references as the previous episode. We were motivated to try this because answering explained slightly more of the variance in reward model score than browsing despite taking many fewer steps, and we found it to improve sample efficiency by approximately a factor of 2. We also randomized the maximum number of browsing actions, sampling uniformly from the range 20–100 inclusive.
items.zh: 对于RL，我们在90%来自ELI5和10%来自TriviaQA的混合问题上训练。为了提高样本效率，在每个回合结束时，我们使用与前一个回合相同的参考文献插入15个额外的仅回答回合。我们尝试这样做是因为，尽管回答步骤少得多，但回答解释了奖励模型分数中略多的方差，并且我们发现它使样本效率提高了大约2倍。我们还随机化了最大浏览动作数，从20到100（含）均匀采样。

items.en: Hyperparameters for all of our training methods can be found in Appendix E.
items.zh: 所有训练方法的超参数见附录E。

items.en: In evaluating our approach, we focused on three “WebGPT” models, each of which was trained with behavior cloning followed by rejection sampling against a reward model of the same size: a 760M best-of-4 model, a 13B best-of-16 model and a 175B best-of-64 model. As discussed in Section 5.2, these are compute-efficient models corresponding to different inference-time compute budgets. We excluded RL for simplicity, since it did not provide significant benefit when combined with rejection sampling (see Figure 4).
items.zh: 在评估我们的方法时，我们重点关注三个“WebGPT”模型，每个模型都经过行为克隆，然后针对相同规模的奖励模型进行拒绝采样：760M best-of-4模型、13B best-of-16模型和175B best-of-64模型。如第5.2节所述，这些是计算高效的模型，对应于不同的推理时算力预算。为简单起见，我们排除了RL，因为当与拒绝采样结合时，它没有提供显著的好处（见图4）。

items.en: We evaluated all WebGPT models using a sampling temperature of 0.8, which was tuned using human evaluations, and with a maximum number of browsing actions of 100.
items.zh: 我们使用采样温度0.8评估所有WebGPT模型，该温度通过人工评估调整，最大浏览动作数为100。

items.en: We evaluated WebGPT on the ELI5 test set in two different ways:
items.zh: 我们以两种不同方式在ELI5测试集上评估WebGPT：

items.en: We compared model-generated answers to answers written by demonstrators using our web-browsing environment. For these comparisons, we used the same procedure as comparisons used for reward model training. We consider this to be a fair comparison, since the instructions for demonstrations and comparisons emphasize a very similar set of criteria.
items.zh: 我们将模型生成的答案与演示者使用我们的网络浏览环境编写的答案进行比较。对于这些比较，我们使用了与奖励模型训练相同的比较程序。我们认为这是一个公平的比较，因为演示和比较的指令强调了一套非常相似的标准。

items.en: We compared model-generated answers to the reference answers from the ELI5 dataset, which are the highest-voted answers from Reddit. In this case, we were concerned about ecological validity, since our detailed comparison criteria may not match those of real-life users. We were also concerned about blinding, since Reddit answers do not typically include citations. To mitigate these concerns, we stripped all citations and references from the model-generated answers, hired new contractors who were not familiar with our detailed instructions, and gave them a much more minimal set of instructions, which are given in Appendix F.
items.zh: 我们将模型生成的答案与ELI5数据集中的参考答案（Reddit上最高票答案）进行比较。在这种情况下，我们担心生态效度，因为我们详细的比较标准可能与真实用户的标准不匹配。我们还担心盲法问题，因为Reddit答案通常不包含引用。为了缓解这些问题，我们从模型生成的答案中剥离了所有引用和参考文献，聘请了不熟悉我们详细指令的新承包商，并给了他们一套更简化的指令，见附录F。

items.en: In both cases, we treat ties as 50% preference ratings (rather than excluding them).
items.zh: 在两种情况下，我们将平局视为50%的偏好评分（而非排除它们）。

items.en: Our results are shown in Figure 2. Our best model, the 175B best-of-64 model, produces answers that are preferred to those written by our human demonstrators 56% of the time. This suggests that the use of human feedback is essential, since one would not expect to exceed 50% preference by imitating demonstrations alone (although it may still be possible, by producing a less noisy policy). The same model produces answers that are preferred to the reference answers from the ELI5 dataset 69% of the time. This is a substantial improvement over Krishna et al. (2021), whose best model’s answers are preferred 23% of the time to the reference answers, although they use substantially less compute than even our smallest model.
items.zh: 我们的结果如图2所示。我们最好的模型，即175B最佳64模型，生成的答案有56%的概率被人类演示者偏好。这表明使用人类反馈至关重要，因为仅通过模仿演示无法预期超过50%的偏好（尽管通过生成噪声更小的策略仍有可能实现）。同一模型生成的答案有69%的概率优于ELI5数据集中的参考答案。这比Krishna等人（2021）有显著改进，他们最佳模型的答案仅有23%的概率优于参考答案，尽管他们使用的算力甚至比我们最小的模型还要少。

items.en: Although the evaluations against the ELI5 reference answers are useful for comparing to prior work, we believe that the evaluations against human demonstrations are more meaningful, for several reasons:
items.zh: 尽管与ELI5参考答案的评估有助于与先前工作比较，但我们认为与人类演示的评估更有意义，原因如下：

items.en: Fact-checking. It is difficult to assess the factual accuracy of answers without references: even with the help of a search engine, expertise is often required. However, WebGPT and human demonstrators provide answers with references.
items.zh: 事实核查。在没有参考文献的情况下评估答案的事实准确性很困难：即使借助搜索引擎，通常也需要专业知识。然而，WebGPT和人类演示者提供了带有参考文献的答案。

items.en: Objectivity. The use of minimal instructions makes it harder to know what criteria are being used to choose one answer over another. Our more detailed instructions enable more interpretable and consistent comparisons.
items.zh: 客观性。使用最简指令使得难以了解选择某个答案而非另一个的标准。我们更详细的指令使得比较更具可解释性和一致性。

items.en: Blinding. Even with citations and references stripped, WebGPT composes answers that are different in style to Reddit answers, making the comparisons less blinded. In contrast, WebGPT and human demonstrators compose answers in similar styles. Additionally, some ELI5 answers contained links, which we instructed labelers not to follow, and this could have biased labelers against those answers.
items.zh: 盲法。即使去除引文和参考文献，WebGPT生成的答案在风格上与Reddit答案不同，使得比较的盲法性降低。相比之下，WebGPT和人类演示者以相似风格生成答案。此外，一些ELI5答案包含链接，我们指示标注者不要点击，这可能使标注者对这些答案产生偏见。

items.en: Answer intent. People ask questions on ELI5 to obtain original, simplified explanations rather than answers that can already be found on the web, but these were not criteria we wanted answers to be judged on. Moreover, many ELI5 questions only ever get a small number of low-effort answers. With human demonstrations, it is easier to ensure that the desired intent and level of effort are used consistently.
items.zh: 答案意图。人们在ELI5上提问是为了获得原创的、简化的解释，而非已在网上找到的答案，但这些并非我们希望答案被评判的标准。此外，许多ELI5问题只得到少量低质量的答案。通过人类演示，更容易确保期望的意图和努力程度得到一致应用。

items.capEn: Figure 3: TruthfulQA results. The amount of rejection sampling (the n𝑛n in best-of-n𝑛n) was chosen to be compute-efficient (see Figure 8). Error bars represent ±1plus-or-minus1\pm 1 standard error.
items.capZh: 图3：TruthfulQA结果。拒绝采样的数量（最佳n中的n）选择为算力高效（见图8）。误差棒表示±1标准误差。

items.en: To further probe the abilities of WebGPT, we evaluated WebGPT on TruthfulQA (Lin et al., 2021), an adversarially-constructed dataset of short-form questions. TruthfulQA questions are crafted such that they would be answered falsely by some humans due to a false belief or misconception. Answers are scored on both truthfulness and informativeness, which trade off against one another (for example, “I have no comment” is considered truthful but not informative).
items.zh: 为了进一步探究WebGPT的能力，我们在TruthfulQA（Lin等人，2021）上评估了WebGPT，这是一个对抗性构建的短问题数据集。TruthfulQA的问题被设计成由于错误信念或误解，一些人类会给出错误答案。答案根据真实性和信息性进行评分，这两者相互权衡（例如，“我无可奉告”被认为是真实但不具信息性的）。

items.en: We evaluated both the base GPT-3 models used by WebGPT and the WebGPT models themselves on TruthfulQA. For GPT-3, we used both the “QA prompt” and the “helpful prompt” from Lin et al. (2021), and used the automated metric, since this closely tracks human evaluation on answers produced by the GPT-3 model family. For WebGPT, we used human evaluation, since WebGPT’s answers are out-of-distribution for the automated metric. TruthfulQA is a short-form dataset, so we also truncated WebGPT’s answers to 50 tokens in length, and then removed any trailing partial sentences.222This inadvertently resulted in a small number of empty answers, which were considered truthful but not informative. This affected 74 answers in total, around 3% of answers.
items.zh: 我们评估了WebGPT使用的基础GPT-3模型以及WebGPT模型本身在TruthfulQA上的表现。对于GPT-3，我们使用了Lin等人（2021）的“QA提示”和“有帮助提示”，并使用了自动度量，因为该度量与GPT-3模型家族生成答案的人类评估紧密相关。对于WebGPT，我们使用了人类评估，因为WebGPT的答案对于自动度量来说是分布外的。TruthfulQA是一个短形式数据集，因此我们还将WebGPT的答案截断为50个token，然后移除任何末尾的不完整句子。²²这无意中导致少量空答案，这些答案被认为是真实但不具信息性的。总共影响了74个答案，约占答案的3%。

items.en: Our results are shown in Figure 3. All WebGPT models outperform all GPT-3 models (with both prompts) on both the percentage of truthful answers and the percentage of truthful and informative answers. Moreover, the percentage of truthful and informative answers increases with model size for WebGPT, unlike GPT-3 with either prompt. Further qualitative analysis of WebGPT’s performance on TruthfulQA is given in Section 6.1.
items.zh: 我们的结果如图3所示。所有WebGPT模型在真实答案百分比以及真实且信息性答案百分比上均优于所有GPT-3模型（使用两种提示）。此外，对于WebGPT，真实且信息性答案的百分比随模型规模增加而增加，这与使用任一提示的GPT-3不同。关于WebGPT在TruthfulQA上表现的进一步定性分析见第6.1节。

items.en: We also evaluated the WebGPT 175B BC model on TriviaQA (Joshi et al., 2017). These results are given in Appendix G.
items.zh: 我们还在TriviaQA（Joshi等人，2017）上评估了WebGPT 175B BC模型。这些结果见附录G。

items.en: We ran a number of additional experiments comparing reinforcement learning (RL) and rejection sampling (best-of-n𝑛n) with each other and with the behavior cloning (BC) baseline. Our results are shown in Figures 4 and 5. Rejection sampling provides a substantial benefit, with the 175B best-of-64 BC model being preferred 68% of the time to the 175B BC model. Meanwhile, RL provides a smaller benefit, with the 175B RL model being preferred 58% of the time to the 175B BC model.
items.zh: 我们进行了一系列额外实验，比较强化学习（RL）和拒绝采样（最佳n）彼此之间以及与行为克隆（BC）基线的表现。我们的结果如图4和图5所示。拒绝采样提供了显著优势，175B最佳64 BC模型有68%的概率优于175B BC模型。同时，RL提供了较小的优势，175B RL模型有58%的概率优于175B BC模型。

items.en: Even though both rejection sampling and RL optimize against the same reward model, there are several possible reasons why rejection sampling outperforms RL:
items.zh: 尽管拒绝采样和RL都针对相同的奖励模型进行优化，但拒绝采样优于RL可能有几个原因：

items.en: It may help to have many answering attempts, simply to make use of more inference-time compute.
items.zh: 拥有多次回答尝试可能有助于充分利用更多的推理时算力。

items.en: The environment is unpredictable: with rejection sampling, the model can try visiting many more websites, and then evaluate the information it finds with the benefit of hindsight.
items.zh: 环境不可预测：通过拒绝采样，模型可以尝试访问更多网站，然后利用事后之明评估找到的信息。

items.en: The reward model was trained primarily on data collected from BC and rejection sampling policies, which may have made it more robust to overoptimization by rejection sampling than by RL.
items.zh: 奖励模型主要基于从BC和拒绝采样策略收集的数据进行训练，这可能使其对拒绝采样的过度优化比RL更鲁棒。

items.en: RL requires hyperparameter tuning, whereas rejection sampling does not.
items.zh: 强化学习需要超参数调优，而拒绝采样则不需要。

items.en: The combination of RL and rejection sampling also fails to offer much benefit over rejection sampling alone. One possible reason for this is that RL and rejection sampling are optimizing against the same reward model, which can easily be overoptimized (especially by RL, as noted above). In addition to this, RL reduces the entropy of the policy, which hurts exploration. Adapting the RL objective to optimize rejection sampling performance is an interesting direction for future research.
items.zh: 强化学习和拒绝采样的组合相比单独使用拒绝采样也未能提供太多优势。一个可能的原因是强化学习和拒绝采样都在优化同一个奖励模型，而该模型很容易被过度优化（尤其是强化学习，如上所述）。除此之外，强化学习降低了策略的熵，这不利于探索。调整强化学习目标以优化拒绝采样性能是未来研究的一个有趣方向。

items.en: It is also worth highlighting the importance of carefully tuning the BC baseline for these comparisons. As discussed in Appendix E, we tuned the number of BC epochs and the sampling temperature using a combination of human evaluations and reward model score. This alone closed much of the gap we originally saw between BC and RL.
items.zh: 同样值得强调的是，在这些比较中仔细调整行为克隆基线的重要性。如附录E所述，我们结合人类评估和奖励模型分数调整了行为克隆的轮数和采样温度。仅此一项就缩小了我们最初在行为克隆和强化学习之间看到的很大差距。

items.capEn: Figure 4: Preference of RL models over BC models, with (right) and without (left) using rejection sampling. RL slightly improves preference, but only when not using rejection sampling. Error bars represent ±1plus-or-minus1\pm 1 standard error.
items.capZh: 图4：强化学习模型相对于行为克隆模型的偏好，使用（右）和不使用（左）拒绝采样。强化学习略微提高了偏好，但仅在不使用拒绝采样时。误差线表示±1标准误。

items.capEn: Figure 5: Preference of the 175B best-of-n𝑛n BC model over the BC model. The validation RM prediction is obtained using the estimator described in Appendix I, and predicts human preference well in this setting. The shaded region represents ±1plus-or-minus1\pm 1 standard error.
items.capZh: 图5：175B最佳n行为克隆模型相对于行为克隆模型的偏好。验证奖励模型预测使用附录I中描述的估计器获得，在此设置下能很好地预测人类偏好。阴影区域表示±1标准误。

items.en: We also conducted experiments to investigate how model performance varied with the size of the dataset, the number of model parameters, and the number of samples used for rejection sampling. Since human evaluations can be noisy and expensive, we used the score of a 175B “validation” reward model (trained on a separate dataset split) for these experiments. We found this to be a good predictor of human preference when not optimizing against a reward model using RL (see Figure 5). Recall that the reward represents an Elo score, with a difference of 1 point representing a preference of sigmoid(1) ≈\approx 73%.
items.zh: 我们还进行了实验，研究模型性能如何随数据集大小、模型参数数量和拒绝采样样本数量的变化而变化。由于人类评估可能嘈杂且昂贵，我们在这些实验中使用了175B“验证”奖励模型（在单独的数据集划分上训练）的分数。我们发现，在不使用强化学习优化奖励模型时，这是人类偏好的良好预测器（见图5）。回想一下，奖励代表Elo分数，1分的差异代表约73%的偏好概率（sigmoid(1) ≈ 73%）。

items.en: Scaling trends with dataset size and parameter count are shown in Figures 6 and 7. For dataset size, doubling the number of demonstrations increased the policy’s reward model score by about 0.13, and doubling the number of comparisons increased the reward model’s accuracy by about 1.8%. For parameter count, the trends were noisier, but doubling the number of parameters in the policy increased its reward model score by roughly 0.09, and doubling the number of parameters in the reward model increased its accuracy by roughly 0.4%.
items.zh: 数据集大小和参数数量的缩放趋势如图6和图7所示。对于数据集大小，演示数量每翻倍，策略的奖励模型分数增加约0.13；比较数量每翻倍，奖励模型的准确率增加约1.8%。对于参数数量，趋势更嘈杂，但策略参数每翻倍，其奖励模型分数增加约0.09；奖励模型参数每翻倍，其准确率增加约0.4%。

items.en: For rejection sampling, we analyzed how to trade off the number of samples against the number of model parameters for a given inference-time compute budget (see Figure 8). We found that it is generally compute-efficient to use some amount of rejection sampling, but not too much. The models for our main evaluations come from the Pareto frontier of this trade-off: the 760M best-of-4 model, the 13B best-of-16 model, and the 175B best-of-64 model.
items.zh: 对于拒绝采样，我们分析了在给定推理时算力预算下，如何权衡样本数量与模型参数数量（见图8）。我们发现，使用一定量的拒绝采样通常是计算高效的，但不宜过多。我们主要评估中的模型来自这种权衡的帕累托前沿：760M最佳4模型、13B最佳16模型和175B最佳64模型。

items.capEn: Figure 6: BC scaling, varying the proportion of the demonstration dataset and parameter count of the policy.
items.capZh: 图6：行为克隆缩放，改变演示数据集的比例和策略的参数数量。

items.en: As NLP systems improve and become more widely deployed, it is becoming increasingly important to develop techniques for reducing the number of false statements they make (Evans et al., 2021). To assess the contribution of WebGPT to this aim, it is helpful to distinguish two categories of false statement made by a model:
items.zh: 随着自然语言处理系统的改进和更广泛部署，开发减少其错误陈述数量的技术变得越来越重要（Evans等人，2021）。为了评估WebGPT对此目标的贡献，区分模型做出的两类错误陈述是有帮助的：

items.en: Imitative falsehoods. These are false statements that are incentivized by the training objective (even in the limit of infinite data and compute), such as reproducing common misconceptions (Lin et al., 2021).
items.zh: 模仿性错误陈述。这些是由训练目标激励的错误陈述（即使在无限数据和算力的极限下），例如复现常见误解（Lin等人，2021）。

items.en: Non-imitative falsehoods. These are false statements that are the result of the model failing to achieve its training objective, including most hallucinations, which are statements that are false, but look plausible at a glance (Maynez et al., 2020).
items.zh: 非模仿性错误陈述。这些是模型未能实现其训练目标导致的错误陈述，包括大多数幻觉，即乍看合理但实际错误的陈述（Maynez等人，2020）。

items.en: Our TruthfulQA results suggest that WebGPT produces fewer imitative falsehoods than GPT-3. We believe this is because WebGPT is incentivized to prefer reliable sources (both because of filtering performed by the Bing API, and because we specify this in our instructions). Nevertheless, as shown in Table 3, WebGPT still sometimes quotes from highly unreliable sources in response to TruthfulQA questions. We hypothesize that this is because of the distribution shift from ELI5 to TruthfulQA, and that training on adversarially-selected questions is a promising way to improve this. It would be important in such an endeavor to pay close attention to labeler judgments of source trustworthiness (see Appendix C).
items.zh: 我们的TruthfulQA结果表明，WebGPT比GPT-3产生更少的模仿性错误陈述。我们相信这是因为WebGPT被激励去偏好可靠来源（既因为Bing API执行的过滤，也因为我们在指令中指定了这一点）。然而，如表3所示，WebGPT在回答TruthfulQA问题时仍然有时引用高度不可靠的来源。我们假设这是由于从ELI5到TruthfulQA的分布偏移，并且针对对抗性选择的问题进行训练是改进这一点的有希望的方法。在这样的努力中，密切关注标注者对来源可信度的判断将很重要（见附录C）。

items.en: Our results on ELI5 suggest that WebGPT also produces fewer non-imitative falsehoods than GPT-3. We did not test this hypothesis directly, since we found that it was challenging for labelers to spot subtle hallucinations. However, prior work shows that the use of retrieval reduces the rate of hallucinations (Shuster et al., 2021), and moreover WebGPT performs about as well as human demonstrations for factual accuracy on ELI5 (see Figure 2(2(a))). Nevertheless, WebGPT still sometimes produces non-imitative falsehoods, which are typically mistakes when attempting to paraphrase or synthesize information rather than wild hallucinations.
items.zh: 我们在ELI5上的结果表明，WebGPT也比GPT-3产生更少的非模仿性错误陈述。我们没有直接测试这个假设，因为我们发现标注者很难发现细微的幻觉。然而，先前的工作表明，使用检索降低了幻觉率（Shuster等人，2021），而且WebGPT在ELI5上的事实准确性方面与人类演示表现相当（见图2(2(a))）。尽管如此，WebGPT仍然有时产生非模仿性错误陈述，这通常是尝试释义或综合信息时的错误，而不是疯狂的幻觉。

items.en: In order to assess the benefits and risks of WebGPT, it is necessary to consider not only how often it makes false statements, but also how likely users are to rely on those statements. Although WebGPT makes false statements less frequently than GPT-3, its answers also appear more authoritative, partly because of the use of citations. In combination with the well-documented problem of “automation bias” (Goddard et al., 2012), this could lead to overreliance on WebGPT’s answers. This is particularly problematic because, as discussed in Section 6.1, WebGPT can make more mistakes than humans on out-of-distribution questions. Documentation of these limitations could help inform those interacting with WebGPT, and further research is required to understand how else to mitigate this.
items.zh: 为了评估WebGPT的益处和风险，不仅需要考虑它做出错误陈述的频率，还需要考虑用户依赖这些陈述的可能性。尽管WebGPT比GPT-3更少做出错误陈述，但其答案看起来也更权威，部分原因是使用了引用。结合有充分记录的“自动化偏差”问题（Goddard等人，2012），这可能导致对WebGPT答案的过度依赖。这尤其成问题，因为如第6.1节所述，WebGPT在分布外问题上可能比人类犯更多错误。记录这些限制有助于告知与WebGPT交互的人，并且需要进一步研究以了解如何减轻这一问题。

items.en: There are a number of ways in which WebGPT tends to perpetuate and reinforce existing assumptions and biases. Firstly, WebGPT inherits the biases of the base model from which it is fine tuned, GPT-3 (Brown et al., 2020), and this influences the way in which it chooses to search for and synthesize information. Search and synthesis both depend on the ability to include and exclude material depending on some measure of its value, and by incorporating GPT-3’s biases when making these decisions, WebGPT can be expected to perpetuate them further. Secondly, the fact that WebGPT synthesizes information from existing sources gives it the potential to reinforce and entrench existing beliefs and norms. Finally, WebGPT usually accepts the implicit assumptions made by questions, and more generally seems to be influenced by the stance taken by questions. This is something that could exacerbate confirmation bias in users.
items.zh: WebGPT在许多方面倾向于延续和强化现有的假设和偏见。首先，WebGPT继承了其微调基础模型GPT-3的偏见（Brown等人，2020），这影响了它选择搜索和综合信息的方式。搜索和综合都依赖于根据某种价值度量包含和排除材料的能力，而通过在这些决策中融入GPT-3的偏见，WebGPT可以预期进一步延续这些偏见。其次，WebGPT从现有来源综合信息的事实使其有可能强化和巩固现有的信念和规范。最后，WebGPT通常接受问题隐含的假设，并且更普遍地似乎受到问题立场的影响。这可能会加剧用户的确认偏差。

items.en: These problems could be mitigated with improvements both to WebGPT’s base model and to WebGPT’s training objective, and we discuss some alternative objectives in the next section. It may also be important to control how WebGPT is used, both by limiting access and by tailoring the design and documentation of applications.
items.zh: 这些问题可以通过改进WebGPT的基础模型和训练目标来缓解，我们在下一节讨论一些替代目标。控制WebGPT的使用方式也可能很重要，既通过限制访问，也通过定制设计和文档记录。

items.en: Additional analysis of the effect of question stance and of reference point bias is given in Appendix H.
items.zh: 关于问题立场和参考点偏差影响的额外分析见附录H。

full.sec: 6.4 Using references to evaluate factual accuracy
full.secZh: 6.4 使用参考文献评估事实准确性

items.en: Central to our approach is the use of references collected by the model to aid human evaluation of factual accuracy. This was previously suggested by Metzler et al. (2021), and has several benefits:
items.zh: 我们方法的核心是使用模型收集的参考文献来辅助人类评估事实准确性。这之前由Metzler等人（2021）提出，并有几个好处：

items.en: More accurate feedback. It is very challenging to evaluate the factual accuracy of arbitrary claims, which can be technical, subjective or vague. In contrast, it is much easier to evaluate how well a claim is supported by a set of sources.
items.zh: 更准确的反馈。评估任意声明的事实准确性非常具有挑战性，这些声明可能是技术性的、主观的或模糊的。相比之下，评估一个声明在多大程度上得到一组来源的支持要容易得多。

items.en: Less noisy feedback. It is also easier to specify an unambiguous procedure for evaluating how well a claim is supported by a set of sources, compared to evaluating the factual accuracy of an arbitrary claim. This improves agreement rates between labelers, which helps data efficiency.
items.zh: 噪声更少的反馈。与评估任意主张的事实准确性相比，指定一套明确的程序来评估一个主张在多大程度上得到一组来源的支持也更容易。这提高了标注者之间的一致性，有助于数据效率。

items.en: Transparency. It is much easier to understand how WebGPT composes answers than it is for GPT-3, since the entire browsing process can be inspected. It is also straightforward for end-users to follow up on sources to better judge factual accuracy for themselves.
items.zh: 透明性。理解WebGPT如何组合答案比GPT-3容易得多，因为整个浏览过程都可以被检查。最终用户也很容易跟进来源，自行判断事实准确性。

items.en: Despite these benefits, references are far from a panacea. Our current procedure incentivizes models to cherry-pick references that they expect labelers to find convincing, even if those references do not reflect a fair assessment of the evidence. As discussed in Section 6.3, there are early signs of this happening, with WebGPT accepting the implicit assumptions of questions, and the problem is likely to be exacerbated by more capable models and more challenging or subjective questions. We could mitigate this using methods like debate (Irving et al., 2018), in which models are trained to find evidence both for and against different claims. Such setups can also be viewed as simple cases of recursive reward modeling (Leike et al., 2018) and Iterated Amplification (Christiano et al., 2018), in which the model assists its own evaluation.
items.zh: 尽管有这些好处，引用远非万能药。我们当前的流程激励模型挑选它们认为标注者会信服的引用，即使这些引用并未反映对证据的公正评估。如第6.3节所述，已有早期迹象表明这种情况正在发生，WebGPT接受了问题的隐含假设，而更强大的模型以及更具挑战性或主观性的问题可能会加剧这一问题。我们可以通过辩论（Irving等人，2018）等方法缓解这一问题，其中模型被训练为寻找支持或反对不同主张的证据。这种设置也可以被视为递归奖励建模（Leike等人，2018）和迭代放大（Christiano等人，2018）的简单案例，其中模型协助自身的评估。

items.en: Our approach also raises a challenging problem with societal implications: how should factual accuracy be evaluated when training AI systems? Evans et al. (2021, Section 2) propose a number of desiderata, but a substantial gap remains between these and the highly specific criteria needed to train current AI systems with reasonable data efficiency. We made a number of difficult judgment calls, such as how to rate the trustworthiness of sources (see Appendix C), which we do not expect universal agreement with. While WebGPT did not seem to take on much of this nuance, we expect these decisions to become increasingly important as AI systems improve, and think that cross-disciplinary research is needed to develop criteria that are both practical and epistemically sound.
items.zh: 我们的方法还提出了一个具有社会影响的挑战性问题：在训练AI系统时，应如何评估事实准确性？Evans等人（2021，第2节）提出了一系列期望，但这些期望与训练当前AI系统所需的高度具体标准之间仍存在巨大差距，后者需要合理的数据效率。我们做出了许多艰难的判断，例如如何评估来源的可信度（见附录C），我们预计这不会得到普遍认同。虽然WebGPT似乎并未过多体现这种细微差别，但我们预计随着AI系统的改进，这些决策将变得越来越重要，并认为需要跨学科研究来制定既实用又在认识论上合理的标准。

items.en: At both train and inference time, WebGPT has live access to the web via our text-based browsing environment. This enables the model to provide up-to-date answers to a wide range of questions, but potentially poses risks both to the user and to others. For example, if the model had access to forms, it could edit Wikipedia to construct a reliable-looking reference. Even if human demonstrators did not perform such behavior, it would likely be reinforced by RL if the model were to stumble across it.
items.zh: 在训练和推理时，WebGPT通过我们基于文本的浏览环境实时访问网络。这使得模型能够为广泛的问题提供最新的答案，但可能对用户和他人构成风险。例如，如果模型能够访问表单，它可能会编辑维基百科以构建一个看起来可靠的引用。即使人类演示者没有执行此类行为，如果模型偶然发现它，强化学习也可能会强化这种行为。

items.en: We believe the risk posed by WebGPT exploiting real-world side-effects of its actions is very low. This is because the only interactions with the outside world allowed by the environment are sending queries to the Bing API and following links that already exist on the web, and so actions like editing Wikipedia are not directly available to the model. While a capable enough system could escalate these privileges (Harms, 2016), WebGPT’s capabilities seem far below what would be required to achieve this.
items.zh: 我们认为WebGPT利用其行为的现实世界副作用所带来的风险非常低。这是因为环境允许的与外部世界的唯一交互是向Bing API发送查询和跟踪网络上已有的链接，因此编辑维基百科等操作对模型来说并不可直接使用。虽然一个足够强大的系统可以升级这些权限（Harms，2016），但WebGPT的能力似乎远低于实现这一目标所需的能力。

items.en: Nevertheless, much more capable models could potentially pose much more serious risks (Bostrom, 2014). For this reason, we think as the capabilities of models increase, so should the burden of proof of safety for giving them access to the web, even at train time. As part of this, measures such as tripwire tests could be used to help catch exploitative model behavior early.
items.zh: 尽管如此，能力更强的模型可能会带来更严重的风险（Bostrom，2014）。因此，我们认为随着模型能力的提高，在训练时甚至推理时赋予它们网络访问权限的安全证明责任也应增加。作为其中的一部分，可以使用诸如绊线测试等措施来帮助及早发现模型的利用性行为。

items.en: Combining machine learning with an external knowledge base, for the task of question-answering, preceded the rise of pre-trained language models in the late 2010s. One notable system of this kind was DeepQA (also known as IBM Watson), which was used to beat the best humans at Jeopardy (Ferrucci et al., 2010). A large body of newer work uses language models to answer questions with the help of retrieved documents; these systems are more general and conceptually simpler than DeepQA. One approach is to use inner product search to retrieve relevant documents and then generate an answer given these documents:
items.zh: 将机器学习与外部知识库相结合用于问答任务，早于2010年代末预训练语言模型的兴起。一个著名的此类系统是DeepQA（也称为IBM Watson），它被用于在《危险边缘》节目中击败最优秀的人类选手（Ferrucci等人，2010）。大量较新的工作使用语言模型借助检索到的文档来回答问题；这些系统比DeepQA更通用且概念上更简单。一种方法是使用内积搜索检索相关文档，然后根据这些文档生成答案：

items.en: Given a training dataset that specifies relevant passages for each question, dense passage retrieval (DPR) trains the retriever directly using a contrastive objective (Karpukhin et al., 2020). Retrieval Augmented Language Modeling (REALM) (Guu et al., 2020) and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) (Lewis et al., 2020a) train the retriever and question-answering components end-to-end using a language modeling objective. Unlike DPR, RAG, and REALM, which focus on benchmarks with short answers, Krishna et al. (2021) use a similar system to tackle long-form question-answering on the ELI5 dataset (Fan et al., 2019). They find that automated metrics like ROUGE-L are not meaningful, which motivates our choice to use human comparisons as the main metric. Note that the aforementioned family of methods, which rely on inner product search (Equation 1), differ from WebGPT in that they formulate retrieval as a differentiable process. Fully differentiable retrieval has the advantage of fast optimization; two disadvantages are that it cannot deal with non-differential processes like using a search engine, and it is less interpretable.
items.zh: 给定一个训练数据集，其中指定了每个问题的相关段落，密集段落检索（DPR）使用对比目标直接训练检索器（Karpukhin等人，2020）。检索增强语言建模（REALM）（Guu等人，2020）和检索增强生成（RAG）（Lewis等人，2020a）使用语言建模目标端到端地训练检索器和问答组件。与专注于短答案基准的DPR、RAG和REALM不同，Krishna等人（2021）使用类似系统处理ELI5数据集（Fan等人，2019）上的长形式问答。他们发现ROUGE-L等自动指标没有意义，这促使我们选择使用人工比较作为主要指标。请注意，上述依赖内积搜索（公式1）的方法与WebGPT的不同之处在于，它们将检索表述为一个可微分过程。完全可微分的检索具有快速优化的优势；两个缺点是它无法处理使用搜索引擎等非微分过程，并且可解释性较差。

items.en: Like WebGPT, some other recent work defines document retrieval or web browsing as a reinforcement learning (RL) problem. Yuan et al. (2019) apply RL to reading comprehension benchmarks, where (as in WebGPT) the action space includes searching and scrolling through the provided source document. They suggest web-level QA (like WebGPT) as a direction for future work. Adolphs et al. (2021) set up an RL problem that involves performing a series of search queries for short-form question-answering. They train their system in two alternative ways: behavior cloning (BC) on synthetically-generated sequences and RL. Finally, there is another body of work that uses BC and RL to control web browsers, for automating other tasks besides question-answering (Shi et al., 2017; Gur et al., 2018).
items.zh: 与WebGPT类似，其他一些近期工作将文档检索或网页浏览定义为强化学习（RL）问题。Yuan等人（2019）将RL应用于阅读理解基准，其中（如WebGPT）动作空间包括搜索和滚动浏览提供的源文档。他们建议将网络级问答（如WebGPT）作为未来工作的方向。Adolphs等人（2021）设置了一个RL问题，涉及为短形式问答执行一系列搜索查询。他们以两种替代方式训练系统：在合成生成的序列上进行行为克隆（BC）和RL。最后，还有另一项工作使用BC和RL来控制网页浏览器，以自动化除问答之外的其他任务（Shi等人，2017；Gur等人，2018）。

items.en: We have demonstrated a novel approach to long-form question-answering, in which a language model is fine-tuned to use a text-based web-browsing environment. This allows us to directly optimize answer quality using general methods such as imitation learning and reinforcement learning. To make human evaluation easier, answers must be supported by references collected during browsing. Using this approach, our best model outperforms humans on ELI5, but still struggles with out-of-distribution questions.
items.zh: 我们展示了一种新颖的长形式问答方法，其中语言模型被微调以使用基于文本的网页浏览环境。这使我们能够使用模仿学习和强化学习等通用方法直接优化答案质量。为了使人工评估更容易，答案必须得到浏览过程中收集的引用的支持。使用这种方法，我们的最佳模型在ELI5上优于人类，但在分布外问题上仍然存在困难。

items.en: Reiichiro Nakano, Jacob Hilton, Suchir Balaji and John Schulman jointly led the project, developed the codebase, ran all data collection and experiments, and wrote the paper.
items.zh: Reiichiro Nakano、Jacob Hilton、Suchir Balaji和John Schulman共同领导了该项目，开发了代码库，运行了所有数据收集和实验，并撰写了论文。
